an10.24:1.1: On one occasion Ven. Mahā Cunda was staying among the Cetis at Sahajāti.
an10.24:1.4: “Yes, friend,” the monks responded to him.
an10.24:2.1: “When a monk utters words about knowing, saying,
an10.24:2.2: ‘I know this Dhamma; I see this Dhamma,’
an10.24:2.3: but he remains conquered by greed,
an10.24:2.12: or evil longing, then it should be known of him that,
an10.24:2.13: ‘This venerable one does not discern how it is that, when one discerns, greed does not come into being, which is why he remains conquered by greed.
an10.24:2.14: This venerable one does not discern how it is that, when one discerns, aversion does not come into being…
an10.24:2.22: evil longing does not come into being, which is why he remains conquered by evil longing.’
an10.24:3.1: “When a monk utters words about developing, saying,
an10.24:3.2: ‘I am developed in body, developed in virtue, developed in mind, developed in discernment,’
an10.24:3.3: but he remains conquered by greed,
an10.24:3.12: or evil longing, then it should be known of him that,
an10.24:3.13: ‘This venerable one does not discern how it is that, when one discerns, greed does not come into being, which is why he remains conquered by greed.
an10.24:3.14: This venerable one does not discern how it is that, when one discerns, aversion…
an10.24:3.22: evil longing does not come into being, which is why he remains conquered by evil longing.’
an10.24:4.1: “When a monk utters words about knowing & developing, saying,
an10.24:4.2: ‘I know this Dhamma; I see this Dhamma; I am developed in body, developed in virtue, developed in mind, developed in discernment,’
an10.24:4.3: but he remains conquered by greed,
an10.24:4.12: or evil longing, then it should be known of him that,
an10.24:4.13: ‘This venerable one does not discern how it is that, when one discerns, greed does not come into being, which is why he remains conquered by greed.
an10.24:4.14: This venerable one does not discern how it is that, when one discerns, aversion…
an10.24:4.22: evil longing does not come into being, which is why he remains conquered by evil longing.’
an10.24:5.1: “It’s just as if a person, though poor, were to talk about riches, or a person without wealth were to talk about wealth, or a person without property were to talk about property,
an10.24:5.2: and then when there arose one situation or another calling for wealth and he would not be able to come forth with wealth or commodities or silver or gold,
an10.24:5.3: then they would know about him that,
an10.24:5.4: ‘This venerable one, though poor, talks about riches; without wealth, he talks about wealth; without property, he talks about property.
an10.24:5.6: Because when there arises a situation calling for wealth, he can’t come forth with wealth or commodities or silver or gold.’
an10.24:6.1: ”In the same way, when a monk utters words about knowing… about developing… about knowing & developing, saying,
an10.24:6.2: ‘I know this Dhamma; I see this Dhamma; I am developed in body, developed in virtue, developed in mind, developed in discernment,’
an10.24:6.3: but he remains conquered by greed,
an10.24:6.12: or evil longing, then it should be known of him that,
an10.24:6.13: ‘This venerable one does not discern how it is that, when one discerns, greed does not come into being, which is why he remains conquered by greed.
an10.24:6.14: This venerable one does not discern how it is that, when one discerns, aversion…
an10.24:6.22: evil longing does not come into being, which is why he remains conquered by evil longing.’
an10.24:7.1: “But when a monk utters words about knowing… about developing… about knowing & developing, saying,
an10.24:7.2: ‘I know this Dhamma; I see this Dhamma; I am developed in body, developed in virtue, developed in mind, developed in discernment,’
an10.24:7.3: and he remains not conquered by greed,
an10.24:7.12: or evil longing, then it should be known of him that,
an10.24:7.13: ‘This venerable one discerns how it is that, when one discerns, greed does not come into being, which is why he remains not conquered by greed.
an10.24:7.14: This venerable one discerns how it is that, when one discerns, aversion…
an10.24:7.22: evil longing does not come into being, which is why he remains not conquered by evil longing.’
an10.24:10.1: “It’s just as if a rich person were to talk about riches, or a wealthy person were to talk about wealth, or a propertied person were to talk about property,
an10.24:10.2: and then when there arose one situation or another calling for wealth and he would be able to come forth with wealth or commodities or silver or gold,
an10.24:10.3: then they would know about him that,
an10.24:10.4: ‘This venerable one, being rich, talks about riches; being wealthy, he talks about wealth; being propertied, he talks about property.
an10.24:10.6: Because when there arises a situation calling for wealth, he can come forth with wealth or commodities or silver or gold.’
an10.24:11.1: “In the same way, when a monk utters words about knowing… about developing… about knowing & developing, saying,
an10.24:11.2: ‘I know this Dhamma; I see this Dhamma; I am developed in body, developed in virtue, developed in mind, developed in discernment,’
an10.24:11.3: and he remains not conquered by greed,
an10.24:11.12: or evil longing, then it should be known of him that,
an10.24:11.13: ‘This venerable one discerns how it is that, when one discerns, greed does not come into being, which is why he remains not conquered by greed.
an10.24:11.14: This venerable one discerns how it is that, when one discerns, aversion…
an10.24:11.22: evil longing does not come into being, which is why he remains not conquered by evil longing.’”
